svg.title: OriGENE CNN architecture
svg.desc: Structural diagram of the OriGENE model showing input tracks, average pooling, convolutional layers, inception module, pooling, flatten, MLP, and sigmoid output.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What epigenetic markers are used as input tracks in OriGENE?')
sendPrompt('Why is matched normal tissue included as a separate track?')
sendPrompt('What additional histone marks can OriGENE use beyond H3K4me3?')
sendPrompt('Why does OriGENE use average pooling before the conv layers?')
sendPrompt('What do the Conv1D layers learn in OriGENE?')
g.text: Local motif detection across bp sequence

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the inception module work in OriGENE and why is it useful?')
g.text: Parallel filter banks — multi-scale feature capture

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is the role of pooling layers after the inception module?')
sendPrompt('What does the flatten operation do in OriGENE?')
sendPrompt('How many dense layers does OriGENE use in the classification head?')
sendPrompt('What does the sigmoid output of OriGENE represent?')
